identity.short_name: Order-8 / QR lock + Vieta-jumping square quotient (p=449,q=617)

problem_spec.summary: Fixed primes p=449 and q=617 (both 1 mod 8) define a canonical least x with ord_p(x)=ord_q(x)=8. With N=x^4+1 and u the least square root of -1 mod N, a constrained factor pair (a,b) of N-1 is rigidified by b \equiv u a (mod N). The induced integer k=(a^2+b^2)/(ab+1) is forced to be a square by Vieta jumping / infinite descent, and the output is t mod 1000 where t^2=k.
givens: p=449 and q=617 are primes with p<q and p \equiv q \equiv 1 (mod 8)
x is the least positive integer with ord_p(x)=ord_q(x)=8
u is the least positive integer with u^2 \equiv -1 (mod N)
ask: Prove there exists exactly one ordered pair (a,b) satisfying the givens and that k=(a^2+b^2)/(ab+1) is a perfect square.
Let t\inN be defined by k=t^2. Report t mod 1000 (as an integer in {0,1,...,999}).

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: prime modulus supporting elements of order 8

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).domain: odd prime with q\equiv1 (mod 8), q>p, q\geq41
parameter(s).purpose: second prime modulus; together with p forces a genuine two-prime order constraint

parameter(s).name: order_target
parameter(s).purpose: structural order target used in defining x

parameter(s).name: canonical_choice
parameter(s).purpose: deterministic canonicalization for selecting x and u

parameter(s).name: ordering_convention
parameter(s).purpose: breaks (a,b)↔(b,a) symmetry to enforce uniqueness of the selected pair

invariant(s).name: order8_forces_x4_cong_minus1
invariant(s).statement: ord_p(x)=8 and ord_q(x)=8 imply x^4 \equiv -1 (mod p) and x^4 \equiv -1 (mod q), hence p|N and q|N for N=x^4+1.

invariant(s).name: QR_minimal_root
invariant(s).statement: For N=x^4+1, the least positive solution of r^2 \equiv -1 (mod N) is u=x^2.

invariant(s).name: anti_triviality_bounds_on_a
invariant(s).statement: From ab+1=N=x^4+1 and 1<a<b we have ab=x^4, hence a^2<ab=x^4 and therefore a<x^2.

invariant(s).name: congruence_implies_divisibility
invariant(s).statement: If b \equiv u a (mod N) and u^2 \equiv -1 (mod N), then N|(a^2+b^2).

invariant(s).name: Vieta_involution
invariant(s).statement: From a^2+b^2=k(ab+1), viewing as a quadratic in b gives a second integer root b'=ka-b; the transformation (a,b)↦(a,b') preserves k.

invariant(s).name: descent_measure
invariant(s).statement: Under the involution, one can decrease max{a,b} unless at a minimal solution, enabling minimal-counterexample / infinite-descent arguments.

essential_assumption(s): Positivity of a,b is essential to run descent on a well-ordered measure such as max{a,b}.
Canonical choices "least positive x" and "least positive u" are essential for determinism.
Output is the positive square root t\inN; once k is proven a square, this t is unique.
uniqueness_certificate(s):
For fixed (p,q), x is unique by "least positive" selection. With N=x^4+1 and u the least root of -1 mod N, the constraints 1<a<b, ab+1=N, and b \equiv u a (mod N) force a<x^2 and hence b=ua=x^2 a (since 0<b<N); then ab=x^4 implies a=x and b=x^3.
Vieta jumping / infinite descent forces k=(a^2+b^2)/(ab+1) to be a perfect square, so t is uniquely determined and hence t mod 1000 is unique.

technique(s).name: discriminant_method
technique(s).purpose: derive the second integer root and the Vieta involution

technique(s).name: Vieta_jumping
technique(s).purpose: run a descent to force k to be a perfect square

technique(s).name: minimal_counterexample
technique(s).purpose: apply the jump to contradict minimality

technique(s).name: quadratic_residue_step
technique(s).purpose: control the correct square-root-of(-1) class and build order-8 witnesses via QR

technique(s).name: multiplicative_order_construction
technique(s).purpose: define the canonical least x with ord_p(x)=ord_q(x)=8

technique(s).name: QR_ORDER_interaction_kernel
inputs: u least root of -1 mod N
technique(s).purpose: use the ORDER-determined shape of N and QR-minimality of u to rigidify (a,b) and compute t

theorem(s).name: infinite_descent
theorem(s).role: formalizes the impossibility of an infinite strictly decreasing sequence of positive integers under the Vieta jump

theorem(s).name: Euler_criterion
theorem(s).role: computes (3/p) and (3/q) via 3^{(r-1)/2} (mod r), bridging residues to exponentiation

theorem(s).name: Lagrange_theorem_on_group_orders
theorem(s).role: guarantees that multiplicative orders modulo a prime divide r-1, supporting order-8 constraints

theorem(s).name: quadratic_reciprocity
theorem(s).role: evaluates Legendre symbols (3/p) and (3/q) via reduction to (p/3) and (q/3)

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Symmetric quadratic Diophantine constraints yield a quadratic with an integer involution (Vieta jumping), while modular reduction locks the admissible factor pair.

critical_trick(s): Use quadratic reciprocity + Euler's criterion to build explicit order-8 residues and determine the canonical least x.
Use QR-minimality of u=x^2 to turn b \equiv u a (mod N) into equality, forcing (a,b)=(x,x^3).
Use Vieta jumping with a minimal-counterexample / infinite-descent argument to force k to be a perfect square.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: modular inverses / CRT, multiplicative order, and factorization for divisor-pair enumeration

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: integer square root and gcd for square checks and normalization

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason:
Correctness checks:
- Enumerate residues r mod p and mod q with r^4 \equiv -1 to obtain the order-8 sets; CRT-combine to find the least x.
- Compute N=x^4+1 and verify u=x^2 is the least positive solution of u^2 \equiv -1 (mod N).
- Enumerate all divisor pairs (a,b) with ab=x^4 and check that exactly one pair satisfies 1<a<b and b \equiv u a (mod N).
- Compute k and confirm k is a perfect square; extract t and t mod 1000.
NT5 preflight attempts:
- Small-mod-only probes with mods in {2,3,4,5,7,8,9,11} do not determine t mod 1000 without recovering x.
- A bounded scan for x up to 50000 finds no integer with ord_p(x)=ord_q(x)=8, so x is not discoverable by a tiny scan.
- Template guesses (p, q, p+q, q-p, pq, 2p+q, p+2q mod 1000, and modular inverses when defined) do not match t mod 1000.
- "Answer-collision resistance: the second-smallest CRT solution gives a different residue mod 1000."

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason:
Independent verification:
- Brute-force scan 2 \leq n < pq for the minimal n with n^4 \equiv -1 (mod p) and n^4 \equiv -1 (mod q), confirming the same x.
- Recompute N,u,(a,b),k,t from that brute-found x and confirm the same t mod 1000.
